title: The Electric Lady Studio Guitar - Klarman & Zaugg
content: The Jimi Hendrix statue, titled ***The Electric Lady Studio Guitar*** and sculpted by Daryl Smith in 1997, stands as a powerful tribute to one of Seattle’s most iconic figures. The statue’s striking presence serves as a visual celebration of Hendrix’s profound impact on the world of music and his enduring legacy in his hometown. Positioned prominently in a busy area of the Pike-Pine Corridor, the statue draws both locals and tourists, inviting them to pause and reflect on the monumental influence of Hendrix’s work. It’s more than just a tribute to a rock legend; it’s a reminder of the city’s role as a crucible for musical innovation.
